description: Zero-capability isolation boundary for the in-app browser's untrusted content webviews (labels `browser-content-*`, created by browser_new_tab on Windows). Grants NO permissions — no core:*, no app commands, no IPC bridge — so hostile web content loaded here can invoke nothing on the powerful Rust backend (vault FS, media server, qBittorrent, PTY). Belt-and-suspenders: these labels are already excluded from the `default` capability's explicit webview list; this makes the zero-grant boundary explicit and prevents a future capability from accidentally globbing content webviews in. The real network boundary is the loopback-refusing proxy the content view is routed through.
